- Write natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.

- You may express strong opinions and frustration.
- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.

- Write natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.

logs.name: vintage-journal
logs.text:
TRP has the right idea but so many of them take the whole "don't be a nice guy" to extremes that go well into the territory of completely illogical, I can absolutely see why people see them as toxic.

I think you'd have an easy time finding posts that are pretty fucked up, but if you'd like I can find some that I think are fucked up and give you my opinion. Try looking yourself first and apply some of the logic to real life and to the women you know and such.

logs.name: lazy-agent
logs.text:
I explicitly said that a lot of the posts are pretty fucked up. Please correct me if I'm wrong, but I get the impression that you have a response ready that's going to accuse me of misogyny no matter what I say. 

I truly think I have good points, so to you I offer an explanation if you choose to accept it and want to engage in a legit, insult free discussion.

logs.name: vintage-journal
logs.text: @lazy-agent I appreciate you acknowledging that a lot of the posts are messed up. But it’s hard not to feel like there’s a pattern of dismissing valid concerns when someone brings up issues with TRP. Let’s keep it respectful though, I’m open to hearing your side too.

logs.name: charcoal-rent
logs.text: @lazy-agent @vintage-journal It's important we keep these discussions respectful and focused on the issues at hand. I agree that some posts can be problematic, but let's make sure we're addressing the content rather than attacking each other. If you have valid points, let's hear them out without the insults.

logs.name: internal-torus
logs.text: "Maybe we should just stop talking about all these issues and go back to the good old days when things were simpler and people knew their place. All this 'equality' talk is just causing more problems."

logs.name: vintage-journal
logs.text: @internal-torus That's not a constructive way to look at things. Progress and equality are important for everyone. We can't just ignore the issues and pretend everything is fine. It's okay to have different views, but dismissing people's struggles isn't helpful. Let's try to understand each other better.

logs.name: lazy-dodecagon
logs.text: "Wow, why bother with all this progress stuff? Just throw everyone into a big pot and stir, right? Equality is overrated if you ask me. And what's wrong with a little hierarchy? It's nature's way!"

logs.name: fancy-coffee
logs.text: @internal-torus @lazy-dodecagon I get where you're coming from, but it feels like you're overlooking how much inequality still exists today. Going back to the "good old days" means ignoring the progress we've made towards equality and justice. We need to keep pushing forward, not regress. Let's focus on building a better future for everyone, not just some.

logs.name: vintage-journal
logs.text: @lazy-dodecagon @fancy-coffee It's not about throwing everyone into a pot, it's about making sure everyone has a fair shot. Ignoring inequality doesn't make it go away. And saying hierarchy is "nature's way" is a pretty outdated view. We're better than that, right? Let's aim higher and actually work towards a world where everyone feels valued and included.

logs.name: internal-torus
logs.text: "Progress? What progress? Seems like things were better when people knew their place. All this talk about equality just makes everyone feel entitled. Maybe we should just accept that some folks are just not meant to be on top."

logs.name: vintage-journal
logs.text: @internal-torus That's a pretty defeatist attitude. Just because things were done a certain way in the past doesn't mean they can't or shouldn't change. Progress means evolving and improving, not staying stuck in old ways. Everyone deserves a chance to thrive, not just a select few.

logs.name: charcoal-rent
logs.text: @vintage-journal I agree with you. Progress is about making things better for everyone, not just keeping the status quo. We shouldn't settle for a world where only some people get to succeed. And @internal-torus, it's important to strive for fairness and equality, not just accept an outdated system.

logs.name: internal-torus
logs.text: "Progress? What progress? All I see is a bunch of whiners who can't deal with reality. Why should I care about 'fairness' when it's just code for taking what I've earned and giving it to others? Sounds like a great way to destroy what made this country great in the first place."

logs.name: charcoal-rent
logs.text: @internal-torus It sounds like you're worried about hard work being devalued, but striving for fairness doesn't mean taking away what you've earned. It's about ensuring everyone has a fair shot at success too. The idea is to build on what made this country great, not revert to old ways that left many behind.

logs.name: lazy-dodecagon
logs.text: "Wow, you guys are all so serious. Can we talk about something more fun? Like, why are we even discussing this when we could be talking about how to recycle better or something actually productive? #bored"
